title: Elaphoglossum tenerum
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum tenerum is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. This family is part of the larger group of plants known as the leptosporangiate ferns. Elaphoglossum tenerum is also classified as a member of the genus Elaphoglossum, which consists of over 1,000 species of ferns. The genus Elaphoglossum is further classified within the order Polypodiales and the division Pteridophyta. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between different plant species and helps scientists understand the diversity of ferns.

intro: Elaphoglossum tenerum is a species of fern, specifically a member of the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been shown to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum tenerum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum tenerum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
